verbs.usageHint: ser + noun/adj — identity/essential quality; ser de — be from / made of / belong to.
estar + adj (temporary) / + location; estar + gerund — progressive; estar de — be in role/mood.
ir a + inf — near future; ir a + place — go to; ir en (car/bus) — go by; irse — to leave.
tener + obj — to have; tener que + inf — have to; tener ganas de — feel like.
hacer + obj — to do/make; hace + time + que — (ago); hacerse + noun/adj — to become.
poder + inf — to be able to; no poder con — can't stand/handle.

assignments.translation: I used to be able to
you used to be able to
he/she used to be able to
we used to be able to
they used to be able to

verbs.usageHint: decir algo a alguien — to tell; decir que + indic (report) / + subj (command).
venir a + inf — come to do; venir de — come from; venir con — come with.
ver + obj — to see (personal 'a' with people); verse con — to meet with.
dar algo a alguien — to give; darse cuenta de — to realize; dar con — to find/come across.
saber + inf — to know how to; saber a — to taste like; saber de — know about.
querer + inf — want to; querer a — love (personal 'a'); querer que + subj — want someone to.
llegar a — to arrive at; llegar a + inf — to manage to; llegar tarde — arrive late.
pasar por — pass by/through; pasar de — not care about; pasar a + inf — go on to do.
poner + obj — to put; ponerse + adj — to become; ponerse a + inf — start to.
quedar con — to meet up with; quedar en + inf — to agree to; quedarse — to stay.
dejar de + inf — to stop doing; dejar que + subj — to let; dejar a alguien — leave someone.
volver a + inf — to do again; volver de — come back from; volverse + adj — to become.
traer + obj — to bring; traer algo a alguien — bring someone something.
seguir + gerund — to keep doing; seguir a alguien — to follow (personal 'a').
encontrar + obj — to find; encontrarse con — to meet/run into.
llamar a alguien — to call someone (personal 'a'); llamarse — to be named.
empezar a + inf — to start doing; empezar por — start by/with.
salir de — to leave from; salir con — to go out with/date; salir a + inf — go out to.
vivir en — to live in; vivir de — live off/on; vivir con — live with.
sentir + obj / que — to feel/be sorry; sentirse + adj — to feel (emotion).
buscar + obj — no 'for' preposition; use personal 'a' for people.
entrar en (Spain) / entrar a (LatAm) — to enter; entrar por — come in through.
trabajar en — work at/in; trabajar de/como — work as; trabajar con — work with.
terminar de + inf — to finish doing; terminar con alguien — break up with.
ayudar a + person; ayudar a + inf — to help someone do.
contar algo a alguien — to tell; contar con — to count on; contar hasta — count to.
cambiar de (tema, ropa, opinión) — to change; cambiar A por B — exchange.
amar a + person — requires personal 'a'.
necesitar + obj/inf — direct, no preposition.
mantener + obj — to maintain; mantenerse + adj — to stay/keep oneself.
pensar en — think about (someone/something); pensar de — think of (opinion); pensar + inf — plan to.
conocer a — to know/meet a person (personal 'a' always).
tomar + obj — to take/have (drink); tomar en cuenta — take into account.
existir — no direct object; often paired with 'hay' in everyday speech.
entender + obj — understand; entender de — to know about (topic).
pedir algo (no prep) — ask for; pedir a alguien que + subj — ask someone to.
mirar + obj — to look at (no preposition); mirar a alguien — look at someone.
escribir a alguien — to write to; escribir sobre/de — write about.
escuchar + obj — direct object (no 'to'); escuchar a alguien — listen to someone.
responder a — to respond to; responder con — respond with.
comprar algo a alguien — can mean buy FROM or FOR someone (context).
abrir + obj (no prep); abrirse a — to open up to.
cerrar + obj — to close; cerrarse — to close itself; cerrar con llave — lock.
subir a — to get on/go up to; subir por — go up via.

assignments.translation: if I were to go up
if you were to go up
if he/she were to go up
if we were to go up
if they were to go up

verbs.usageHint: bajar de — to get off/out of; bajar a — to go down to.

assignments.translation: if I were to go down
if you were to go down
if he/she were to go down
if we were to go down
if they were to go down
